title: add epoch for specfile
body:
to get the copr rpms to jive better with the fedora rpms, we need to set an epoch.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

For posterity, the particular problem this is solving is that the `podman` rpms in Fedora have `Epoch: 2`:

```
$ yum info podman
Name         : podman
Epoch        : 2
Version      : 1.8.0
Release      : 2.fc31
```

The packages in the COPR repository had no epoch, so despite being a more recent build of podman they were always considered a downgrade from the stock Fedora packages (so `dnf upgrade` would always replace them).

It's  possible to resolve this by giving the COPR a lower repository priority (e.g., adding `priority=50` to `/etc/yum.repos.d/_copr:copr.fedorainfracloud.org:baude:Upstream_CRIO_Family.repo`).

This commit addresses the issue in the packages by setting `Epoch: 99` when building fedora packages, ensuring that the COPR packages will always be considered an upgrade from the stock packages.

Looks good to me.

@larsks TYVM for the addon explanation!  Luv it!
LGTM
